details.issue: loadImageFromBytes has no timeout — can hang analysis loop forever if PNG frame never fires onload/onerror
ff.on(progress) handler never removed — accumulates across repeated runs causing stale callbacks
catch block does not clean WASM FS files — stale input.mp4/frame.png/highlight.mp4 left behind on error, breaking second run
resultUrl blob URL never revoked when user discards result — memory leak proportional to video size

e2e_test.output: 15s highlight generated, video player and download button both present

details.issue: resultUrl blob URL never revoked on discard/unmount — memory leak proportional to video size

feature_step: add test feedback UI: 1-5 star rating + failure-type checkboxes after highlight generation, saves to localStorage

details.issue: photoPreview blob URL never revoked on photo change or discard — memory leak accumulates across repeated photo uploads

feature_step: BGM toggle: generated 30s sport beat (470KB), added UI toggle, FFmpeg cut replaces audio with BGM when enabled, cleanup on both success and error paths

e2e_test.output: 15s highlight generated with BGM, video player shows 0:15, feedback panel present

details.issue: fetchFile("/bgm/sport1.mp3") had no timeout — could hang indefinitely in WeChat WKWebView when BGM is enabled

feature_step: beat-sync: findBestWindow now nudges startT so peak action lands on BGM measure downbeat (every 2s at 120 BPM) when bgmEnabled; no effect when BGM off

detail.notes: Full flow: video upload → player photo upload → BGM enabled → highlight generated. All steps completed without freeze or error.

bugs_fixed.desc: bgmEnabled missing from useCallback deps — if user toggles BGM after uploading files, run() sees stale value; fixed by adding bgmEnabled to dep array

features: sliding_window_scoring: findBestWindow now sums scores across the 15s window (cumulative prefix sum) instead of centering on peak frame — favors sustained player+ball activity over lucky single-frame matches
confidence_gradient: analyzeFrame now weights frame score by blob match quality (bestScore/150), giving 0.5-1.0x multiplier — clear detections contribute more than marginal ones
detail.notes: Previous cycle e2e (jhb3.mp4 + BGM) confirmed pipeline stable. These improvements change scoring math only, type-checked clean.

details.issue: Zombie FFmpeg instance on CDN timeout: 30s CDN + 60s local fallback ran as parallel downloads, congesting mobile bandwidth. Fixed with 5s HEAD probe to choose CDN vs local before starting any download.

photoUrl blob URL not revoked when img.onerror fires — outer catch handler skips the revokeObjectURL call. Added try/finally so URL is always revoked. Also added 10s timeout to guard against WKWebView never firing onload/onerror.

bugs_fixed.desc: fetchFile('/bgm/sport1.mp3') silently failed in production — catch block swallowed error, hasBgm stayed false, original audio played. Replaced with generateBeatWAV(): pure in-browser 120BPM WAV synthesis using 180Hz kick/350Hz snare/900Hz hi-hat/440Hz synth. No network dependency, no failure path. Also fixes zombie bgm.mp3 file in WASM FS.

detail.notes: Waiting for Railway deploy (~2min) before running production e2e.

detail.mode: single_window_fallback (jhb1.mp4 is 20s, too short for multi-segment)

bugs_fixed.verified: BGM in-browser WAV generation confirmed working on production — fetchFile was the real failure path, now eliminated

bugs_fixed.desc: findHighlightSegments() now collects all player-has-ball events; for longer videos (>30s) this produces non-continuous concatenated clips instead of one window containing unrelated players

detail.methodology_note: Root cause of missed bugs: all prior testing on localhost where WASM loads in <1s masking real mobile network issues; BGM fetch failure only visible on production. Fix: production e2e now mandatory every 3 cycles per qa_mission.txt; in-browser BGM generation eliminates entire class of network-dependent failures.

details.issue: ff.on('progress', onProgress) registered before ff.exec() but ff.off() only called on success path — if ff.exec() throws (filter_complex error, seek-past-end, etc.) the listener stays attached to the cached FFmpeg instance. Repeated failed runs accumulate stale closures that fire setProgress with stale values on subsequent encodes. Fixed by wrapping ff.exec() calls in try/finally to guarantee ff.off() always runs.

feature_step: align single-window BGM path to use amix 0.3/0.7 (was silently replacing all game audio)

details.issue: Single-window+BGM used '-map 1:a' which dropped original game audio entirely and played only the synthesized beat. Multi-segment path was already using amix 0.3/0.7 correctly. Mission spec says '原声低 + BGM高'. Fixed: changed single-window to use filter_complex amix so both paths produce 30% original + 70% BGM. Needs human test to verify audible mix in production.

fixes_verified.desc: amix=inputs=2:weights=0.3|0.7 — pipe separator replaces space; no 处理失败 error on production with BGM enabled, fix confirmed
Both single-window and multi-segment paths now use amix 0.3/0.7; game audio is preserved at 30%
try/finally ensures ff.off() always called — accumulating listener bug eliminated

multi_segment.triggered: unknown — 15s output consistent with single-window fallback; no console debug logs in production build; jhb2.mp4 events may be sparse enough to fall back to single window
multi_segment.note: Need human test on longer video (>60s) with continuous ball-handling action to confirm multi-segment concat path

details.issue: canvas.getContext('2d') called on every frame loop iteration without willReadFrequently:true. Each getImageData call triggered a GPU→CPU readback — confirmed by 'Multiple readback operations' console warning seen in production. For a 55s video (55 frame iterations), this means 55 GPU readbacks instead of 0. On WeChat/mobile this adds significant latency to each frame and risks hitting the 90s overall timeout on longer videos. Fix: hoisted getContext before the loop with willReadFrequently:true so canvas stays CPU-backed. Also eliminates the repeated getContext overhead (was creating/looking up the context object 55+ times per run).

totalFrames = Math.ceil(duration * SAMPLE_FPS) had no upper bound. A 10-minute video at SAMPLE_FPS=1 produces 600 frame extractions; each ff.exec call on mobile takes ~1-2s, so the analysis loop would run 10-20 minutes guaranteed to hang — WeChat WKWebView would kill the page. Mission explicitly calls out '>10min' as a required scenario. Fix: cap totalFrames at 90 and use frameInterval=duration/totalFrames so sampling is proportional. Videos ≤90s keep 1s interval (unchanged); longer videos sample at wider intervals covering the full video. findBestWindow and findHighlightSegments are frame-rate-agnostic (use scores[i].t timestamps) so they work correctly with the new intervals.

details.wasm_load: fast — well under 60s budget (CDN path likely used)
details.notes: Frame cap fix behavioral effect not testable with 20s video (20 < 90); willReadFrequently is a transparent performance improvement. Both commits deployed and pipeline healthy. BGM+amix stable.

details.issue: Error catch block (introduced with BGM real-music fetch in ee3da1b) hardcoded deleteFile('bgm.wav') but the new real-music path writes 'bgm.mp3' to WASM FS. If fetchFile('/bgm/music.mp3') succeeded but ff.exec then threw, bgm.mp3 was leaked in WASM FS — not cleaned up on error. On repeated failure runs this accumulates stale files in the 31MB WASM heap, risking OOM on WeChat. Fix: add parallel deleteFile('bgm.mp3') attempt in catch block so both possible BGM filenames are cleaned up on any failure path.

feature_step: tester badge incentive: write tester_badge to localStorage on feedback submit + show unlock message

details.note: Added localStorage.setItem('tester_badge','true') to feedback submit handler. Updated thank-you message to show '🏅 测试员徽章已解锁'. Badge persists across sessions; profile-page integration can read it in a future cycle. Needs human test to verify UI message appears after submitting feedback.

bugs.desc: ff.exec() returns exit code but never throws — opaque ErrnoError: FS error when encode fails. Fixed: check return code and throw with message.
useMultiSeg had no totalSegDur cap. jhb2.mp4 detected player in 51/55 frames → 51s total segments + 4.5MB BGM WAV in 31MB WASM heap → OOM. Fixed: guard totalSegDur <= HIGHLIGHT_S * 2.
generateBeatWAV called with totalClipDur+2 with no upper bound. Capped at HIGHLIGHT_S+5 to keep WAV ≤ 1.8MB.

fetchFile('/bgm/music.mp3') resolved on HTTP 404, writing Railway HTML page as bgm.mp3. FFmpeg tried to decode HTML as MP3: [mp3] Failed to read frame size / Invalid argument. Fixed: use fetch()+resp.ok check, throw on non-2xx so fallback to WAV synthesis triggers.

detail.root_cause_note: All 4 bugs only visible with jhb2.mp4 (15.6MB) + BGM — jhb1.mp4 (4.2MB) has enough WASM headroom to mask the heap pressure, and the fetchFile 404 bug only manifests when music.mp3 is absent from production.

details.issue: extractPlayerSignature called ctx.getImageData 4+ times on a canvas created without willReadFrequently:true. Triggered GPU->CPU readbacks + Chrome warning on every run. Fix: add willReadFrequently:true to getContext call at line 93 (same fix previously applied to the frame analysis loop in 5f69831).

detail: tester badge display on profile page: useEffect reads localStorage 'tester_badge', renders 🏅 内测员 badge in grid when present
notes: no new stability bugs found in full page.tsx review; dead code: try/catch around ff.exec in frame loop never triggers (ff.exec doesn't throw), real guard is readFile catch — harmless; blob URL not revoked on 重新制作 — minor leak, non-critical

detail: replace dead try/catch around ff.exec in frame loop with exit-code check — catch never fired since ff.exec returns int not throws; now consistent with encode path pattern from 7ed9473
notes: also confirmed: blob URL leaks already fixed (useEffect cleanup at lines 445-451); canvas.height correctly set before drawImage in frame-0 path (if(i===0) block runs before ctx.drawImage)

detail.wasm_load_note: already at 74% frame analysis within ~10s of click — WASM loaded fast on production

detail: hoist BFS direction array to module-level constant DIRS4 — both jersey-blob BFS and motion-blob BFS in analyzeFrame were recreating a 4-element array literal on every BFS step, causing millions of small allocations per run on long videos; WeChat WKWebView (JSC) has stricter GC timing than V8 and stalls under allocation pressure
notes: no other stability bugs found in full code review; generateBeatWAV logic correct; findBestWindow edge cases all handled; findHighlightSegments zero-duration segment handled by 0.8s padding

detail: user-provided music file as BGM tier-1 source — 3-tier BGM chain: (1) user-uploaded audio ≤3MB, (2) /bgm/music.mp3 bundled track, (3) WAV synth fallback; BGM card shows file-picker when toggle is on; ✕ clears selection; toggle-off resets selection; bgmUserFile added to run() dependency array
notes: human test: enable BGM, upload a ≤3MB MP3, generate — should hear user music in output; >3MB file silently falls through to WAV synth

detail.bgm_source: WAV synth (tier-3 fallback — no music.mp3, no user file)
detail.multiseg: single-window-fallback (totalSegDur>30s guard active — OOM fix confirmed)

detail: add 180s overall analysis deadline — per-frame has 8s image-load timeout but no total cap; 90×8s=720s worst case on WeChat WKWebView where user sees no progress for 12min; deadline breaks loop early with message '分析超时，已处理 N/M 帧，继续生成…' and proceeds with partial scores; findBestWindow/findHighlightSegments both handle empty/partial arrays gracefully

multi-frame voting: post-processing pass after analysis loop suppresses isolated hasPlayer=true where both neighbors are false — reduces same-jersey false positives that would anchor bad highlight segments; score reduced to 5% instead of 0 so the frame still contributes weakly to window scoring
notes: human test: with a video where teammates have similar jerseys, isolated detections should no longer cause highlight to be cut around a random teammate; needs comparison run with and without the change

detail.wasm_heap_note: 246.6MB file wrote to MEMFS successfully — Emscripten MEMFS is JS-heap backed not WASM-heap limited; prior 31MB OOM was encode-time working-set (decode+BGM buffers), not file storage

detail: show inline warning when BGM user file exceeds 3MB size limit — file was silently skipped in favor of WAV synth with no user feedback; user heard drum beat and had no way to know their music was ignored; now filename shown in red with size and reason (X MB 超限，将用内置节拍) immediately after file selection
notes: next cycle 32: e2e test candidate — consider testing with user-provided BGM file to verify the warning renders and correct tier-2/3 fallback; cycle 33 will be mandatory e2e; remaining feature candidates: (1) large video >100MB processing time warning, (2) status msg distinction first-load vs cached WASM load

detail: show processing time warning for large videos >100MB — cycle 30 e2e showed DJI 246MB took 85s with no user hint on expected duration; warning appears inline below the video picker immediately after file selection showing estimated 1-2 min
notes: cycle 33 is mandatory e2e test; pending stability: multi-seg no-audio crash (low probability for sports videos but real); consider 52_raw.MP4 or jhb3.mp4 for cycle 33 variety

detail.wasm_note: already cached — loading phase was near-instant

notes: next mandatory e2e: cycle 36; pending stability: multi-seg no-audio crash (low real-world probability but real crash path); next feature candidates: (1) no-audio video crash fix in multi-seg + single-window-BGM paths, (2) WASM cached vs first-load status message distinction

detail: prevent crash on videos with no audio track — probe step now detects audio stream presence via Stream.*Audio: log pattern; useMultiSeg gains && hasAudio guard (concat v=1:a=1 requires audio from every input — would exit non-zero on silent video); single-window BGM path split into 3-way conditional: (1) source+BGM amix when hasAudio, (2) BGM-only map when !hasAudio, (3) no BGM
notes: low real-world probability for sports videos but a real crash path (exit non-zero → user sees 编码失败 error); fix is purely defensive; next cycle 35: feature — consider WASM cached vs first-load status message distinction OR start bundling a real BGM track in public/bgm/music.mp3

detail: fix tier-2 BGM always 404 — public/bgm/sport1.mp3 (470KB) was already bundled but fetch URL was /bgm/music.mp3 (wrong filename); guaranteed 404 every time meant bundled track was completely unused and BGM always fell through to WAV synth tier-3; one-line URL fix restores the real sport track as tier-2

details.issue: fetch URL /bgm/music.mp3 does not match bundled file /bgm/sport1.mp3 — tier-2 BGM permanently dead since sport1.mp3 was added

notes: needs human test: enable BGM, generate highlight — should now hear real sport1.mp3 track instead of WAV drum synth; cycle 36 is mandatory e2e — good time to validate this with BGM enabled

detail.bgm_source: sport1.mp3 tier-2 [HTTP 200 confirmed via network log]

notes: sport1.mp3 confirmed HTTP 200 in production — cycle 35 fix validated; human still needs to listen to output to confirm music sounds correct vs WAV synth; next mandatory e2e: cycle 39; next feature: second bundled BGM track, or beat-sync alignment improvement

feature_step: accurate WASM status message on second run: show 视频引擎已就绪，开始处理… when ffmpegRef.current is set, original 30-60s warning only on fresh load

details.issue: status msg always showed 首次需30-60秒 even on second run where WASM is already cached and load is skipped (~0ms)

title: WASM background preload on mount
detail: Added ensureFFmpegLoaded() useCallback + ffmpegInitRef dedup + mount useEffect. WASM 31MB now downloads silently while user is uploading files; click-to-start delay drops from 30–60s to ~0s on second+ visit or if preload completes before click. Graceful fallback if preload fails (resets ffmpegInitRef so run() retries). TypeScript clean. Pushed to Railway.

details.issue: loadImageFromBytes() at line 621 was awaited outside any try/catch — a single corrupt or timeout-failed PNG frame would propagate to run()'s outer error handler and abort the entire highlight generation even with 89 other valid frames. Fixed by wrapping in try/catch and using continue to skip bad frames gracefully.

e2e_test.wasm_source: cdn.jsdelivr.net [200] — preload worked, no wait after click
e2e_test.notes: WASM preload (cycle 38) confirmed working in production — page started processing immediately after click with no 30-60s wait; next mandatory e2e: cycle 42

details.issue: cycle-39 introduced 'continue' for undecodable frames but left canvas size init guarded by 'if (i===0)'. If frame 0 is skipped, sampleH stays at 16:9 default (135px) and all subsequent frames are drawn squished on a wrong-aspect-ratio canvas. Affects non-16:9 videos (e.g. DJI 4:3) when their first frame happens to be undecodable. Fixed: replaced i===0 check with canvasReady flag — first successfully decoded frame always initializes canvas dimensions.
